title: 7 hidden credit behaviours that may be hurting your credit score
summary: Protect your credit score from silent damage caused by hidden credit behaviours that reduce creditworthiness, from high utilisation to missed payments and credit report errors.

title: TDS on FD interest decoded: Who pays, how much banks deduct, and exemption rules
summary: Fixed deposit interest provides passive income for many Indians but is subject to TDS under the Income Tax Act. TDS is deducted at 10% if interest exceeds ₹50,000, with senior citizens enjoying a higher exemption. Forms 15G and 15H can help avoid TDS.

title: How multiple credit card applications influence your credit report and loan approval chances
summary: Multiple credit card applications can lower your credit score due to repeated hard inquiries, which increase lenders' risk perception, while spaced applications and responsible credit usage help maintain a healthy credit profile.

title: Estate planning: Financial mistakes families make after a loved one’s death — and how to avoid them
summary: The death of a family member disrupts finances, making it crucial to focus on financial matters. Key steps include obtaining a death certificate, listing assets and liabilities, managing insurance claims, and re-planning finances to ensure stability during this difficult time.

title: BlackRock bets big on stablecoins with two new crypto-linked money-market funds | Key details explained
summary: BlackRock Inc. plans to launch two money-market funds for stablecoin investors, including a digital version of its $6.1 billion liquidity fund and a new tokenized fund for crypto wallet users.&nbsp;

title: Tax-saving FDs explained: Lock-in period, tax benefits and banks offering highest interest rates
summary: Fixed deposits remain popular among Indians for their safety and reliable returns. With tax-saving FDs offering deductions up to ₹1.5 lakh and interest rates as high as 7.90%, they are ideal for risk-averse investors seeking stable wealth accumulation.

title: I asked ChatGPT how to fund a Europe trip—SIPs or credit cards? – its answer was practical
summary: To fund a Europe trip, SIPs are safer and predictable, requiring monthly savings of ₹24,000- ₹25,000. Credit cards can be cost-efficient if repayments align with bonuses, but delays can lead to high costs. A hybrid strategy is recommended for cost efficiency.

title: FII ownership hits 14-year low to 14.7%; DII cushions Indian markets with 18.9% rise: Report
summary: Foreign Institutional Investor (FII) ownership as a percentage of total Indian equities has fallen from 19.9 per cent in April 2016 to 14.7 per cent in April 2026, marking its lowest level since June 2012, according to JM Financial's Fundamental Research report.
